title: FamilyRelationships
lean_code_with_comments:
def Pa (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => (w, v) ∈ G.E)

def PaSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Pa

def Ch (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => (v, w) ∈ G.E)

def ChSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Ch

def Sib (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => (v, w) ∈ G.L)

open Classical in
noncomputable def Anc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => ∃ p : Walk G w v, p.IsDirectedWalk)

noncomputable def AncSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Anc

open Classical in
noncomputable def Desc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => ∃ p : Walk G v w, p.IsDirectedWalk)

noncomputable def DescSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Desc

noncomputable def NonDesc (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V) \ G.DescSet A

noncomputable def Sc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  G.Anc v ∩ G.Desc v

noncomputable def ScSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Sc

open Classical in
noncomputable def Dist (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=
  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => ∃ p : Walk G v w, p.IsBidirectedWalk)

noncomputable def DistSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=
  A.biUnion G.Dist

def PaSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=

  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => (v, w) ∈ G.L)

  (G.J ∪ G.V).filter (fun w => ∃ p : Walk G w v, p.IsDirectedWalk)

noncomputable def NonDesc (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=

noncomputable def Sc (G : CDMG Node) (v : Node) : Finset Node :=

noncomputable def ScSet (G : CDMG Node) (A : Finset Node) : Finset Node :=

lean_explanation:
The file formalises items 1–14 of `def_3_5`, the family-relation sets attached to every node (and every set of nodes) of a CDMG. All fourteen declarations live in `namespace CDMG`, so the calls `G.Pa v`, `G.AncSet A`, `G.NonDesc A`, etc. read left-to-right exactly like the LN's $\Pa^G(v)$ and $\Pa^G(A)$. The `G : CDMG Node` first argument is the graph; `v : Node` (unrestricted) is a single vertex; `A : Finset Node` is a set of vertices. The carrier `Node` is the underlying vertex type from `def_3_1`, with `DecidableEq Node` available throughout for finset operations.

Every output of every definition is a `Finset Node`, never a `Set Node`. The single-vertex sets `Pa / Ch / Sib / Anc / Desc / Sc / Dist` are built by `Finset.filter` over the ambient `G.J ∪ G.V` — Lean's literal transcription of the LN's $\{w \in G \mid \ldots\}$. The set-versions add the suffix `Set` (`PaSet`, `AncSet`, etc.) because Lean cannot overload the LN's single $\Pa^G$ symbol; each is `A.biUnion G.Pa` (etc.), Mathlib's idiom for $\bigcup_{v \in A} \Pa^G(v)$ that keeps the result a deduplicated finset rather than a `Set`.

The three walk-based definitions (`Anc`, `Desc`, `Dist`) reuse the `Walk G u w` inductive from `def_3_4` and its `IsDirectedWalk` / `IsBidirectedWalk` predicates: the filter spells `∃ p : Walk G u w, p.IsDirectedWalk`, with arguments swapped for `Anc` versus `Desc` and `IsBidirectedWalk` substituted for `Dist`. This existential has no primitive `Decidable` instance, so each definition is prefixed `open Classical in` and marked `noncomputable`; the status propagates through `AncSet / DescSet / Sc / ScSet / NonDesc / DistSet`. `NonDesc A` is the `Finset.sdiff` complement of `G.DescSet A` inside `G.J ∪ G.V`; `Sc v` is `G.Anc v ∩ G.Desc v` via `Finset.inter`. The LN's self-membership notes ($v \in \Anc^G(v)$, etc.) hold unconditionally because `Walk.nil` from `def_3_4` supplies the trivial length-0 walk, which satisfies both directed and bidirected walk predicates vacuously — no `∪ {v}` patch is needed in any of the four self-inclusive definitions.
design_choices:
**`Finset Node`, never `Set Node`, as the uniform carrier.** Every one of the fourteen sets returns a `Finset Node`. The LN's $\{w \in G \mid \ldots\}$ already implies a filter over the finite vertex universe $J \cup V$, so the natural Lean shape is `Finset.filter` on `G.J ∪ G.V`. Going via `Set Node` would force `Finset.toSet` coercions at every set-version (`NonDesc` uses `Finset.sdiff`, `Sc` uses `Finset.inter`, the set-versions use `Finset.biUnion`) and would lose the `DecidableEq` and `Fintype` instances that chapter-4 CBN factorisations rely on for finset products such as $\prod_{w \in \Pa(v)} p(w \mid \ldots)$.

**Single-vertex defs take `v : Node` unconditionally.** The LN's preamble says "$v, w \in V$" but its set-versions iterate over $A \subseteq J \cup V$. Splitting into a $V$-typed single-vertex signature and a $(J \cup V)$-typed set-version would force `Finset.subtype` plumbing at every call site. Instead, the single-vertex defs accept any `v : Node`; the filter universe `G.J ∪ G.V` automatically returns the empty finset for out-of-graph nodes, and the input/output asymmetry — e.g. `G.Pa j = ∅` for $j \in J$ but $\Ch^G(j)$ may be non-empty — emerges from the edge-typing axioms `hE_subset` and `hJV_disj` rather than from an explicit hypothesis.

**Set-versions are pure `biUnion`, with no set-minus subtraction.** The LN source contains a commented-out alternative $\Pa^G(A) := (\bigcup_{v \in A} \Pa^G(v)) \setminus A$, with the author's own caveat that, under directed self-loops, this would break $\Pa^G(\{v\}) = \Pa^G(v)$. The author rejected the exclusive form, so we follow the inclusive form for all six `…Set` definitions: this gives `G.PaSet {v} = G.Pa v` definitionally, and the set-version and single-vertex version interoperate without case-splitting on self-loops. Downstream proofs needing parents distinct from $v$ must spell `G.PaSet A \ A` at the use site rather than getting it from the definition.

**`noncomputable` for the walk-based families.** `Walk G u w` is an inductive of arbitrary length, so `∃ p, p.IsDirectedWalk` has no primitive `Decidable` instance. We `open Classical in` locally to discharge `Finset.filter`'s `DecidablePred` obligation. A constructive decidable variant is recoverable via bounded BFS (every reachable vertex is reachable by a path of length at most $|J \cup V|$), but no chapter-3 proof needs it, so we pay the `noncomputable` cost upfront. Plain `Pa / Ch / Sib`, whose predicates reduce to Finset membership, stay computable.

**Self-membership flows from `Walk.nil`.** The LN's $v \in \Anc^G(v) / \Desc^G(v) / \Sc^G(v) / \Dist^G(v)$ notes hold via the trivial length-0 walk `Walk.nil hv : Walk G v v`, whose `IsDirectedWalk` and `IsBidirectedWalk` evaluate to `True` vacuously — this is enforced one layer down in `def_3_4`. The $\Dist$ case is the most fragile of the four: CDMGs forbid bidirected self-loops, so without the trivial-walk reading, $v \in \Dist^G(v)$ would fail in any graph with no bidirected edge incident to $v$. The other three cases happen to admit length-$\geq 1$ witnesses in most graphs, but `Dist` cannot.

**No `SibSet`, no single-vertex `NonDesc`.** The LN does not introduce either (the $\Sib$ set-version slot is commented out in the source; $\NonDesc$ appears only as a set operation). We follow the LN literally rather than padding the API; consumers needing the missing forms can spell them inline as `A.biUnion (G.Sib)` or `G.NonDesc {v}`.
